name: Chow Fan
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces Snow peas, fresh or frozen
recipeInstructions:
Note: Chow Fan is best made the day before you plan to serve it.
1. Cut boned chicken into half-inch squares; place in a bowl and sprinkle with half the garlic salt, half the sherry, and half the cornstarch.
2. Cut peeled shrimp into pieces about 1 inch long. Place them in a separate bowl with the remaining garlic salt, sherry, and cornstarch.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons oil over high heat in a wok or large pan. Add onions and celery; stir-fry for 2 minutes.
4. Add soy sauce; reduce heat to medium, cover, and steam for one minute.
5. Add mushrooms; cover and steam for an additional two minutes.
6. Remove the mixture to a large bowl.
7. Add remaining oil to the wok, reduce heat to low, and add ginger and garlic. Brown lightly, stirring constantly to avoid burning.
8. Add chicken and shrimp. Stir-fry for 2 minutes, or until shrimp are pink and opaque.
9. Add cooked rice, onion mixture, and ham; stir until hot. Remove from heat, transfer to a storage container, and chill overnight.
10. When ready to serve, reheat in a wok or large pot. Stir in snow peas and cook for two minutes.
11. Make a well in the center of the wok and stir in the beaten eggs. Stir them as they cook, about 3 minutes, so they scramble and mix with the other ingredients. Cover and cook over medium heat another couple of minutes.
12. Serve immediately.
Serves 12 as part of a family-style Chinese dinner, or 4 as a main dish.
